forward.note: Beat-and-raise cadence; GM marching toward the next leg.

trail.val: Pricing the biggest upside; from ~+9% q/q level, mid-teens price increases in 12 months 'could be'; WDC more price-driven (cost -~10%/EB)
Vendors targeting ~$25/TB blended nearline CY27, ~$30 CY28 vs ~$14.50 today; spot $30-35; WDC CY27/CY28 EPS 'a little over $45 / over $70' if checks hold

forward.note: LTA escalators roughly doubling pricing in two years is the single biggest EPS swing.

trail.val: Supply chain: 'WDC too optimistic on HAMR for 2027'; high-volume HAMR 'not easy'
Pulled one HAMR customer qual forward to 1H26; may bridge gap with another ePMR generation
HAMR-lag worry 'too much in focus'; ePMR cost-optimized to ~60TB; LT margins converge with STX
WDC hasn't qualified HAMR yet; ramps 44TB only H1 CY27 (vs STX 40TB 2H CY26) — lacks near-term pricing/GM juice

forward.note: Pace/feasibility of 2027 HAMR is the core bull/bear fault line vs Seagate.

trail.val: LTAs now extend into CY2028-2029; exabyte-volume commitments with pricing that may reset on new-capacity introductions

forward.note: Lengthening LTA horizon; reset clauses tie pricing to areal-density transitions.

trail.val: UBS caution: TTM EB shipments already above long-term trendline; shadow-order risk + accelerated eSSD replacement
Long-term storage demand framed >25% CAGR; EB +34% y/y; no planned unit-capacity increase (areal-density growth)
Supply capped ~30-35% annual EB growth -> imbalance elongates the cycle rather than adding near-term volume

forward.note: Supply discipline (no unit adds) is what underwrites the multi-year pricing thesis.
